skill.name: stitch-design-taste
skill.description: Creates Stitch-ready DESIGN.md files that define a premium UI design system with typography, color, layout, motion, and anti-pattern rules.
skill.summary: Generates structured design standards for premium Google Stitch screen generation.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the static findings as false positives caused by Markdown inline code, design vocabulary, and a documentation link. No executable scripts, shell command invocation, cryptographic implementation, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md or DESIGN.md.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backticks mark inline Markdown values such as fonts, CSS units, and example design tokens. They are not Ruby backtick execution and do not invoke a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown documents, and the reviewed locations contain prose or inline formatting. No interpreter, script file, command runner, or user-controlled shell input is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Design Text False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain headings, design rules, or sample UI text. No evidence found of cryptographic APIs, hashing algorithms, credential storage, or authentication logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review found design-system prose rather than security-sensitive code. The static pattern appears to match ordinary words, Markdown, or numeric examples.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are UI Vocabulary
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged system-related words describe design systems, layout rules, and viewport testing. No evidence found of host enumeration, environment probing, or filesystem discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms occur inside UI and responsive-design instructions. There are no commands such as uname, whoami, ls, network scanners, or similar reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is a Documentation Link
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged URL points users to Google Stitch as a prerequisite. The skill does not fetch the URL, transmit data to it, or require credentials to be sent there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a Markdown hyperlink in a prerequisites section. No network client code or data exfiltration path exists in the reviewed files.

content.value_statement: Generic AI screens often look repetitive and weak. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured design rules for premium Google Stitch outputs.
actual_capabilities: Creates a structured DESIGN.md format for Google Stitch projects.
Defines color roles with neutral bases, single accents, and banned palettes.
Specifies typography rules, font choices, scale, and readability limits.
Documents component styling for buttons, cards, inputs, loaders, and errors.
Sets responsive layout rules for mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports.
Captures motion intent for later implementation by coding agents.
limitations: It provides design instructions, not executable UI components.
It depends on the user or agent to apply rules consistently in Stitch.
It does not validate generated screens with visual testing tools.
Its aesthetic is opinionated and may not fit conservative brand systems.

use_cases.title: Start a Stitch Project
use_cases.description: Create a design standard before generating the first screen in Google Stitch.

use_cases.title: Align AI-Generated Screens
use_cases.description: Give an AI coding agent clear visual rules before implementing exported Stitch designs.

use_cases.title: Reduce Generic UI Patterns
use_cases.description: Replace common AI layout habits with stronger typography, spacing, and component rules.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Design Standard
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new app idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use stitch-design-taste to create a DESIGN.md for a productivity app. Keep the style balanced, premium, and mobile friendly.

prompt_templates.title: Tune the Visual Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Adjust creative direction before screen generation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Stitch design system for a finance dashboard with low visual noise, strong data hierarchy, and restrained motion.

prompt_templates.title: Document a Product-Specific System
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate rules for an existing product concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a DESIGN.md for a boutique travel marketplace. Include atmosphere, colors, typography, components, layout, motion, and banned patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Handoff to Engineering
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced handoff from Stitch design to implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a detailed Stitch design standard for a SaaS analytics app, then add implementation notes for responsive behavior and motion intent.

output_examples.input: A new creator portfolio needs a premium visual system for Stitch.
output_examples.output: A DESIGN.md outline with gallery-like spacing, one muted accent, distinctive display typography, asymmetric hero rules, and clear banned patterns.

output_examples.input: A dashboard concept needs less generic AI styling.
output_examples.output: A practical design standard with dense information layout, monospace numeric rules, restrained surfaces, skeleton loading states, and mobile collapse rules.

output_examples.input: A team wants motion guidance after exporting Stitch screens.
output_examples.output: A motion section describing spring behavior, staggered reveals, transform-only animation, and performance boundaries for implementation.

best_practices: Set the creativity, density, variance, and motion levels before generating design rules.
Adapt the palette and typography to the product domain instead of accepting defaults.
Use the banned-pattern list as a quality gate during screen review.
anti_patterns: Using the skill as a finished component library or production theme package.
Ignoring responsive rules after generating desktop-focused Stitch screens.
Combining this opinionated style with unrelated brand systems without review.

faq.question: Does this skill generate application code?
faq.answer: No. It generates design guidance that agents can use when creating or implementing screens.

faq.question: Is this only for Google Stitch?
faq.answer: It is optimized for Google Stitch, but the design rules can guide other AI design workflows.

faq.question: Can I change the visual style?
faq.answer: Yes. Adjust the creativity, density, variance, and motion settings before using the rules.

faq.question: Does it support enterprise dashboards?
faq.answer: Yes. Use lower creativity, higher density, monospace numeric rules, and restrained motion.

faq.question: Will it verify the final UI?
faq.answer: No. You still need visual review and responsive testing after screens are generated.

faq.question: Why does it include banned patterns?
faq.answer: The bans help agents avoid common generic UI habits and keep outputs consistent.
